name: Caddy reverse proxy routing
description: Caddy routes /api/* to bridge :3001, /chat-ui/* to OpenClaw :18789, /* to Next.js :3000. Must strip X-Frame-Options and override CSP frame-ancestors for iframe embedding.

title: OpenClaw iframe uses /__openclaw/ path
rationale: Prefix-stripping /chat-ui/ breaks API routing. /__openclaw/ preserves native gateway paths so all API calls route correctly.

description: GetSandboxConfig gRPC needs sandbox_id UUID not name
resolution: Use GetSandbox by name to get UUID, or read policy from GetSandbox spec.policy.network_policies directly.

description: npx openclaw gateway run fails in sandbox because npm registry is blocked before policy presets are applied
resolution: Use /usr/local/bin/diffract gateway run (the renamed openclaw binary already installed in the image) instead of npx

description: meta/llama-4-maverick model ID returns 404 from NVIDIA API
resolution: Correct model ID is meta/llama-4-maverick-17b-128e-instruct. Always verify model IDs against NVIDIA API /v1/models endpoint.

title: Per-sandbox Caddy routes auto-generated via API
rationale: Each sandbox gets /agent/<name>/ route to its gateway port. POST /api/gateway-routes/sync regenerates Caddyfile from openshell forward list and reloads Caddy. Enables multi-sandbox support.

content: nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b is the correct default model for the NVIDIA NIM provider. This is already set in cli/bin/lib/inference-config.js as DEFAULT_CLOUD_MODEL.

description: GetSandboxLogs and WatchSandbox gRPC require sandbox_id UUID, response uses 'logs' field not 'entries', log lines use timestamp_ms not timestamp
resolution: Resolve name to UUID via GetSandbox first. Map response field 'logs' to 'entries' and normalize timestamp_ms to ISO string for UI.

description: Telegram bridge required NVIDIA_API_KEY but never used it — bridge forwards to sandbox which handles inference via inference.local
resolution: Removed NVIDIA_API_KEY requirement from both scripts/telegram-bridge.js and api/routes/channels.js. API keys never leave the host — OpenShell injects them at the network layer.

title: Channels management via host-side bridge processes
rationale: Channel bridges (Telegram, Discord, etc.) run on the VPS host, not inside sandboxes. Managed via /api/channels with start/stop/config. State in /tmp/diffract-channels.json. Verification: bot token validated via Telegram getMe API, sandbox checked via openshell sandbox list CLI.

content: Channel start flow: 1) Validate bot token via platform API (Telegram getMe), 2) Verify sandbox exists and is Ready via openshell CLI, 3) Spawn bridge process, 4) Wait 3s stabilization check before reporting running. Never show running without verification.

Syncing an MCP server to sandboxes requires two steps: 1) Merge the MCP host into the sandbox network policy via UpdateConfig gRPC (not policy set which replaces). 2) Push OpenClaw MCP server config inside sandbox and update integrity hash.

description: OpenClaw config set rejects mcp.servers.* — schema validation fails with 'Unrecognized key: mcp'
resolution: Write MCP config directly to openclaw.json via python, then update .config-hash and fix permissions. The config set CLI validates against a schema that doesn't include mcp.* in OpenClaw 2026.3.11.

name: Gateway restart needed after MCP config change
description: OpenClaw loads MCP server configs at gateway startup only. After writing mcp.servers.* to openclaw.json, the gateway must be restarted. Added POST /api/sandboxes/:name/restart-gateway endpoint and Restart Gateway button in sandbox detail header.

content: When openclaw config set fails with schema validation (unrecognized key), write to /sandbox/.openclaw/openclaw.json directly via python, then sha256sum > .config-hash and chown sandbox:sandbox. Always update hash after direct writes or gateway will log integrity warnings.

description: diffract gateway stop does not reliably kill the gateway process — old process keeps listening on port 18789 so new gateway fails to start
resolution: Use openshell sandbox connect to pipe commands (the documented way): diffract gateway stop, sleep 3, then diffract gateway (not gateway run). The connect pipe handles process lifecycle correctly.

description: MCP sync shell escaping: inline python with JSON breaks through 4 levels of shell nesting (Node→bash→docker exec→bash→python)
resolution: Base64-encode both the python script and the JSON config. Write script to /tmp/merge-mcp.py via base64 decode, then run python3 /tmp/merge-mcp.py <b64-config-arg>. Avoids all quoting issues.

description: gRPC UpdateConfig rejects partial policy — sending only new rules is treated as deleting existing rules
resolution: Must send the FULL policy object (filesystem, landlock, process, all network_policies) with the new rule added. Fetch current via GetSandbox, merge, then UpdateConfig.

content: Docker needs {default-cgroupns-mode: host} in /etc/docker/daemon.json or OpenShell containers fail. Applied automatically during Docker install and checked on existing installs in preflight step.

name: VPS SSH access
description: sshpass -p '24Hrushi@02hp' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o PubkeyAuthentication=no root@187.127.137.249

name: One-command VPS deploy
description: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hrubee/Diffraction/main/scripts/deploy-vps.sh | bash — with config: DIFFRACT_DOMAIN=srv1534809.hstgr.cloud NVIDIA_API_KEY=nvapi-... bash scripts/deploy-vps.sh

name: VPS health check commands
description: Run via SSH: systemctl is-active caddy; ss -tlnp | grep ':(80|443|3000|3001|18789)'; docker ps; openshell sandbox list; openshell forward list; openshell inference get; curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/health; curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:18789/health

name: VPS service restart and route sync
description: systemctl restart caddy; fuser -k 18789/tcp (kill stale gateway); curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/gateway-routes/sync (regenerate Caddy routes after sandbox changes)

name: VPS PATH setup for SSH sessions
description: In SSH: export NVM_DIR="\$HOME/.nvm" && [ -s "\$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && . "\$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" && export PATH="\$PATH:\$HOME/.local/bin" — needed for openshell and node commands

content: scripts/deploy-vps.sh installs ALL prerequisites from bare Ubuntu 24.04, skips what is already present. VPS needs NOTHING pre-installed. One command handles everything end-to-end.

description: Caddy header_up is a subdirective of reverse_proxy, not standalone
resolution: Move header_up inside reverse_proxy block: reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:18789 { header_up Host {hostport} }

content: psmisc package provides the fuser binary on Ubuntu; 'fuser' is not a standalone apt package name

title: Chat route changed to /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat/
rationale: /__openclaw/ was broken and non-descriptive. New route is per-sandbox and follows the dashboard URL structure.

name: port-forward-restart
description: OpenShell port forward can die (PID goes stale). Restart with: openshell forward start <sandbox> <port> or openshell <sandbox> connect. Check with ss -tlnp | grep <port> and openshell forward list.

name: kubectl-exec-sandbox-replacement
description: Replace 'openshell doctor exec -- kubectl exec -n openshell <name> -- <cmd>' with stdin piping: echo B64 | base64 -d | openshell sandbox connect <name>, where B64=Buffer.from(cmd).toString('base64'). Replace kubectl cp with openshell sandbox upload. Keep kubectl for k8s resource ops (patch, rollout, netns) in fix-coredns.sh and setup-dns-proxy.sh.

content: To start the OpenClaw gateway port forward persistently on VPS: openshell forward start 18789 my-assistant --background. The --background flag keeps it alive after SSH disconnect. Without it, the forward dies when the terminal session ends or services restart. Run this after every VPS service restart.

name: gateway-502-dual-check
description: 502 on chat route can be caused by the gateway process dying inside the sandbox while the port forward remains alive. Diagnose by checking both: (1) openshell forward list, (2) openshell sandbox exec <name> -- pgrep -f diffract-gateway. Fix: HOME=/sandbox /usr/local/bin/diffract gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --auth none

name: sqlite-job-table
description: SQLite job table pattern for durable onboard state: api/lib/jobs.js uses node:sqlite (Node >= 22.5, built-in) with WAL mode for concurrent reads. Schema: jobs + job_events tables. Avoids extra deps vs better-sqlite3. API is ESM, CLI is CJS — both can open the same ~/.diffract/jobs.db file directly.

openshell sandbox port forward dies silently. Symptoms: 502 from Caddy on /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat/, 'openshell forward list' shows status dead, gateway itself still alive inside sandbox. Recurring issue tracked as Diffraction-5d78. Took down chat access twice in one session.
resolution: export PATH=/root/.local/bin:$PATH; openshell forward stop <port> <name>; openshell forward start <port> <name> --background. Permanent fix needs watchdog auto-restart (Diffraction-5d78) or surface status in dashboard (Diffraction-e860).

content: On Hostinger VPS (root@187.127.137.249), non-login SSH shells have no PATH for diffract tooling. openshell binary lives at /root/.local/bin/openshell, diffract at /usr/local/bin/diffract. Always prefix SSH commands that invoke these: export PATH=/root/.local/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH before using. systemd services load correct PATH themselves — only non-interactive SSH needs explicit export.

name: caddy-split-static-dynamic
description: Diffract Caddy config splits static + dynamic: /etc/caddy/Caddyfile is the static template (diffraction.in marketing + srv1534809.hstgr.cloud framework), and imports /etc/caddy/conf.d/*.conf for per-sandbox handle blocks. api/routes/gateway-routes.js generateSandboxConf() writes ONLY to conf.d/sandboxes.conf — never touches the main Caddyfile. This prevents race conditions where gateway-routes/sync clobbered diffraction.in block. POST /api/gateway-routes/sync must be called after every onboard (Diffraction-a331). Fixed in commit addd012.

Host-side openshell port-forward dies silently (socat/kubectl port-forward worker killed). Gateway inside sandbox stays alive (Telegram bridge keeps responding — cluster-internal network). Only host 127.0.0.1:<port> tunnel breaks, causing Caddy 502 and blank chat iframe. Confirmed misdiagnosis trap: 'gateway dead' is wrong framing — the gateway is fine, the tunnel is not. Causes: k8s API dropping stream, pod restart, systemd-oomd, loopback hiccup.
resolution: openshell forward stop <port> <sandbox>; openshell forward start <port> <sandbox> --background. Automated via scripts/forward-watchdog.sh polling every 30s. Watchdog must parse SANDBOX BIND PORT PID STATUS columns — use col 3 (PORT) not col 2 (BIND address).

name: openshell-forward-list-columns
description: openshell forward list output schema: SANDBOX BIND PORT PID STATUS (5 columns, whitespace-separated, ANSI color in STATUS). BIND is always the bind address (e.g. 127.0.0.1), PORT is the numeric local port. Shell parsers must read: 'read -r name _bind port _pid status' — col 3 for port, not col 2. Bug in initial forward-watchdog.sh (Diffraction-91bb) grabbed col 2 (127.0.0.1) as port and every revive call failed silently.

content: Diffract API server (node api/server.js on :3001) is started by systemd / bash wrapper with default PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin. execSync('openshell ...') calls from api/routes/sandboxes.js fail unless /root/.local/bin is in PATH OR openshell is symlinked into /usr/local/bin. Workaround deployed: ln -sf /root/.local/bin/openshell /usr/local/bin/openshell. Proper fix tracked as Diffraction-e451 — pass { env: { ...process.env, PATH: '/root/.local/bin:' + process.env.PATH } } to execSync. Symlink survives node restarts but NOT VPS reinstalls.

name: ssh-f-tunnel-silent-death
description: openshell forward start uses ssh -f ProxyCommand via /connect/ssh mTLS gateway; forked ssh keeps TCP listener alive but app-layer tunnel silently breaks (curl exit 52 Empty reply from server) while PID and 'openshell forward list' status both look healthy. PID-only watchdog cannot detect this. Diagnose: curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/ returns empty body. Workaround: bypass openshell forward with docker exec openshell-cluster-diffract kubectl port-forward -n openshell <sandbox> 18789:18789 --address 0.0.0.0 then host-side socat TCP-LISTEN:18789,fork,reuseaddr TCP:172.18.0.2:18789. Permanent fix Diffraction-eb76 (systemd diffract-gateway-bridge@<sandbox>.service supervisor with HTTP probe).

name: gateway-bridge-permanent
description: Permanent fix for silent openshell forward ssh death: run diffract-gateway-bridge.sh under systemd diffract-gateway-bridge@sandbox.service. Discovers cluster container + bridge IP via docker inspect. HTTP probe every 10s restarts kpf+socat on failure. Replaces openshell forward in restart-gateway route and forward-watchdog.
docker exec kubectl port-forward: killing host-side docker exec wrapper does NOT kill inner container process. Port 18789 stays bound across bridge restarts.
resolution: Add docker exec pkill -9 in stop_children AND ExecStop in systemd unit. Bump TimeoutStopSec to 20s.

content: Diffraction repo lives at /root/.diffract/repo on VPS (not /root/Diffraction). API server runs via nohup (not systemd). GitHub origin is https://github.com/hrubee/Diffraction.

From Scrtch/diffract UI deploy flow has 3 stages streamed via SSE to a single form: Stage 1 = scripts/bootstrap.sh (apt deps, Docker+cgroup fix, OpenShell via NVIDIA install.sh, Diffract CLI npm install from /opt/diffract/repo, /usr/local/bin/diffract wrapper). Stage 2 = diffract onboard --non-interactive with DIFFRACTION_* env vars (NOT DIFFRACT_*) — provider must be cloud|ollama|vllm|nim, NVIDIA_API_KEY required for cloud. Stage 3 = scripts/sync-sandbox-routes.sh writes /etc/caddy/conf.d/sandboxes.conf from 'openshell sandbox list', reloads Caddy, installs diffract-gateway-bridge@<name>.service (systemd template) for each active sandbox, retires any stale diffract-forward-watchdog@ (the deprecated ssh-based one). All three stages use values from the form — no hardcoded sandbox names/domains anywhere. Bridge script + template installed on first sync if missing.

name: bridge-pod-gateway-recovery
description:
diffract-gateway-bridge distinguishes 'tunnel alive but upstream gateway dead' from 'tunnel dead'. When HTTP probe fails (code 000) while kpf_alive && socat_alive, restart_pod_gateway() runs: mkfifo pipe | openshell sandbox connect , then pipes in 'pkill -9 -f openclaw-gateway; HOME=/sandbox nohup /usr/local/bin/diffract gateway run --bind loopback --port

--auth token'. Only falls back to rebuilding kpf+socat if pod-restart doesn't recover. Sandbox name comes from systemd template %i — zero hardcoding. See scripts/diffract-gateway-bridge.sh.

Leaked kubectl port-forward inside openshell-cluster-diffract blocks new bridge starts with 'bind: address already in use'. When bridge is killed mid-session, its 'docker exec kubectl port-forward' child leaks inside the cluster container. Leaked kubectl's cmdline can be cleared (shows just 'kubectl' with no args) so argv-grep-based detection misses it.
resolution: In start_bridge() before launching new kpf, look up listening socket inode via /proc/1/net/tcp (match :<port-hex>, state 0A), walk /proc/<pid>/fd for the inode, kill those PIDs. Inode-based detection survives cmdline clearing. See diffract-gateway-bridge.sh start_bridge() leaked-PID block.

name: openclaw-sub-path-mount
description: Mount OpenClaw at /<sandbox>/oc/ by injecting <base href='/<sandbox>/oc/'> + window.__OPENCLAW_CONTROL_UI_BASE_PATH__ into the gateway root HTML before main JS runs. Implementation: Next.js API route (From Scrtch/diffract/src/app/api/oc/[...slug]/route.ts) fetches http://127.0.0.1:18789/ and string-replaces <head>. Caddy routes /<sandbox>/oc exact → rewrite to /dashboard/api/oc/<sandbox> → Next.js. Everything else under /<sandbox>/oc/* proxies direct to gateway. Critical: the WebSocket path is ALSO /<sandbox>/oc (base path IS the WS root in OpenClaw), so a top-level @any_sandbox_ws matcher (path_regexp + Connection Upgrade + Upgrade websocket) must route WS upgrades directly to gateway before the HTML route fires.

name: diffract-onboard-env-vars
description: Diffract onboard CLI non-interactive env var mapping (cli/bin/lib/onboard.js): DIFFRACTION_NON_INTERACTIVE=1, DIFFRACTION_ACCEPT_THIRD_PARTY_SOFTWARE=1, DIFFRACTION_PROVIDER ∈ {cloud, ollama, vllm, nim} (NOT openai/anthropic/gemini directly — those all map to 'cloud' for now), DIFFRACTION_MODEL=<id>, DIFFRACTION_SANDBOX_NAME=<name>, DIFFRACTION_POLICY_MODE=custom, DIFFRACTION_POLICY_PRESETS=comma,separated, NVIDIA_API_KEY for cloud, DIFFRACTION_ENDPOINT_URL for custom endpoints. Prefix is DIFFRACTION_ (full word) not DIFFRACT_ — common mistake that silently falls back to interactive prompts.

kubectl port-forward + socat bridge (mulch mx-cf2dfc) does not work against OpenShell sandbox pods. openclaw-gateway listens INSIDE a nested openshell sandbox netns, not the outer pod's default netns. kubectl port-forward targets the pod's outer namespace, so it forwards to 127.0.0.1:18789 there — which is empty. The bridge shows 'Bridge up' in logs but every request gets 'Connection refused'. Compounded by an EADDRINUSE respawn loop when recovery logic kills openclaw-gateway: the supervisor (pid 168, comm openclaw) instantly respawns it racing our nohup start. Wasted ~1 hour debugging before abandoning.
resolution: Retire the bridge. Use a simpler diffract-forward-watchdog that wraps openshell forward (which traverses the nested netns correctly via SSH into the sandbox) + HTTP probe for silent-death detection. See scripts/diffract-forward-watchdog.sh and the Stage 3 in sync-sandbox-routes.sh that installs + enables the systemd template per sandbox. The only cost vs bridge: when onboard's built-in forward has silent death, watchdog restarts it (3 consecutive HTTP probe failures) — slower recovery than a kpf+socat bridge would have been IF the bridge had worked.

name: forward-watchdog-http-probe
description: Replace the PID-only openshell forward watchdog (mulch mx-74b5e3 — PID exists but app-layer tunnel is dead, probe fails but check thinks it's fine) with an HTTP probe-based one. scripts/diffract-forward-watchdog.sh runs 'openshell forward start <port> <sandbox> --background' once, then every 10s curls http://127.0.0.1:<port>/health with a 3s timeout. Anything non-2xx counts as a failure. After FAIL_THRESHOLD=3 consecutive failures: stop+start the forward. Installed as systemd template diffract-forward-watchdog@.service and instantiated per sandbox by sync-sandbox-routes.sh. Fully generic — sandbox name comes from %i. Does NOT kill openclaw-gateway; trusts the pod's own supervisor.

Linux TASK_COMM_LEN is 15 chars — 'openclaw-gateway' (16 chars) gets truncated to 'openclaw-gatewa' in /proc/<pid>/comm and in process listings. This breaks every naive 'pkill -9 openclaw-gateway' and 'pkill -9 -x openclaw-gateway' call: they match on comm (short name) and miss the actual worker. The supervisor (pid 168, comm literally 'openclaw') keeps respawning the worker. Symptom: gateway restart scripts report success but the target process stays alive.
resolution: Always use 'pkill -9 -f openclaw-gateway' (matches against full /proc/<pid>/cmdline, not the truncated comm) OR use the short truncated name 'pkill -9 openclaw-gatewa'. For Diffract specifically: prefer 'diffract gateway stop' which talks to the supervisor and releases the lock cleanly — that avoids the kill-rename issue entirely.

name: nextjs-standalone-timeout-patch
description: Next.js 16 standalone server.js uses http.createServer with Node defaults: requestTimeout=300s, headersTimeout=60s. These cut off long-running requests like 5GB streaming uploads or long SSE deploy streams. Patch script (scripts/patch-standalone-timeouts.sh) is idempotent via a marker '// __DIFFRACT_TIMEOUT_PATCH__' on line 1 and prepends: wraps http.createServer to set server.requestTimeout=0, server.headersTimeout=0, server.timeout=0. Must be applied after 'npm run build' and before 'systemctl restart diffract-ui'. Run as part of every build pipeline: build → cp .next/static to .next/standalone/.next/static → cp public → cp scripts → run this patch.

plugins/diffract-core/dist/ is a build artifact required by the sandbox Dockerfile (COPY diffract/dist/ /opt/diffract/dist/) during 'diffract onboard' step 'Creating sandbox'. When rsyncing the Diffract repo to a VPS with --exclude='dist/' or --exclude='**/dist/', the plugin dist gets stripped, and onboard fails with 'cp: cannot stat plugins/diffract-core/dist/'. The error surfaces deep in the Docker build output so it's easy to misread as a build-context issue.
resolution: Two options: (A) scope the dist/ exclude pattern so it only matches top-level target/dist, not plugins/<name>/dist — tricky with rsync. (B) Auto-build plugins in bootstrap: if plugins/<name>/dist is missing or empty, run 'npm install && npm run build' in that plugin dir. scripts/bootstrap.sh now does (B) as step 5b — iterates plugins/*/ and builds each with missing dist. Idempotent (skips if dist already populated).

content: Files destination inside the sandbox is /sandbox/uploads/ (flat, no folders for v1). Created on first /api/files/list call by scripts/ensureUploadDir which runs 'mkdir -p && chown 998:998' via docker exec kubectl exec. UID 998 = sandbox user. Users upload via the /dashboard/files page; the AI agent reads from /sandbox/uploads/ inside the pod. Files survive sandbox restart but die with sandbox destroy.

title: Two-layer uninstall: sandbox-only default keeps /dashboard always-on
rationale: User requirement: https://<host>/dashboard must stay reachable even when sandboxes are torn down, so users can redeploy through the form. Three modes: default (sandbox + bridge + cluster container), --full (also removes UI + CLI source), --purge (wipes Node/Docker/Caddy/OpenShell back to a blank VPS). --sandbox <name> allows surgical single-sandbox removal with Caddy route regeneration. No hardcoded sandbox names anywhere in uninstall.sh; lookups come from 'openshell sandbox list'.

content: openshell binary install lives at /root/.local/bin/openshell on Hostinger VPS but non-login SSH shells have PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:... — missing ~/.local/bin. Unlike diffract (which has a shim at /usr/local/bin/diffract that re-exports PATH and execs the real binary), openshell ships with no shim. Fix both: ln -sf /root/.local/bin/openshell /usr/local/bin/openshell (immediate) AND append 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' to /root/.bashrc (permanent for future binaries). Without either, 'openshell sandbox list' fails with command not found even though the 16MB binary is installed and executable.

name: xrdp-gui-launch-from-ssh
description: Launch GUI app inside user's active xrdp session from a remote SSH shell: (1) find the xrdp Xorg display by grepping 'ps -eo pid,user,cmd' for 'Xorg :N' where user matches xrdp session owner — root typically ends up on :10, :11, or :12 depending on session order; (2) /root/.Xauthority holds the magic cookie; (3) launch with 'env DISPLAY=:N XAUTHORITY=/root/.Xauthority google-chrome-stable --no-sandbox --new-window URL'. Chrome requires --no-sandbox when running as root. Use nohup + disown so window survives ssh exit. Verified on Ubuntu 24.04 + XFCE4 xrdp.

name: installer-systemd-idempotent
description: install.sh captures OLD_HEAD before git pull and NEW_HEAD after; sets CODE_CHANGED=1 if they differ or if fresh clone. For each systemd unit, renders template to tmp file and uses cmp -s to compare with installed unit; sets UNIT_CHANGED=1 only if different. Services are restarted only when UNIT_CHANGED=1, CODE_CHANGED=1, or service is not active — otherwise left running.

content: ui/next.config.ts has no standalone output configured, so systemd ExecStart must use node INSTALL_DIR/ui/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next start -p 3000 -H 127.0.0.1 — NOT .next/standalone/server.js.
diffract-ui systemd service needs /var/lib/diffract-ui owned by the service User for credentials marker writes; set DIFFRACT_UI_CREDS_PATH env var to point there
